hawkish_phrases: inflation was still expected to remain too high for too long
it was safer to decide on an additional hike, given the highly uncertain environment and the significant disinflation that was still required
A higher interest rate would more strongly limit the amplification of any upside shocks to the inflation path
erring on the side of pausing the first time the decision was a close call could risk being interpreted as a weakening of the ECB's determination
an additional consideration was the risk that the inflation path embedded in the projections was fragile and additional supply shocks could push inflation further above the target for longer
dovish_phrases: downside risks to economic growth could be significant
a lot of the pass-through of past rate hikes was still pending
economic momentum was expected to pick up, as real incomes were expected to rise
the risks of hiking at the present time, and later having to reverse course should the economy weaken by more than expected, were larger
summary: The Governing Council raised the three key ECB interest rates by 25 basis points, a close call decision with a solid majority supporting the hike, emphasizing that inflation remains too high for too long despite weaker economic growth and strong monetary transmission already underway.
